lines: Let's begin by arriving. [long pause] Not rushing into anything. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] arriving. [long pause] Letting whatever you were doing a minute ago fall away. [long pause] Letting the noise of the day quiet, just a little.
Take a slow breath in through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five.

And out through your mouth, gently... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven.
Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] filling your lungs slowly, letting your chest and belly expand... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five.

And out... [long pause] [long pause] releasing, like a long sigh... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven.
One more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] slowly... [long pause] [long pause]
And out... [long pause] [long pause] all the way to the bottom of the breath.

Let your breathing find its own rhythm now. [long pause] You don't need to control it. [long pause] Just notice the gentle rise and fall. [long pause] Your body breathing itself. [long pause] A gift you didn't ask for.

Feel your body in this moment. [long pause] The weight of you. [long pause] The way you're sitting or lying — the contact points where your body meets the surface beneath it. [long pause] Your back. [long pause] Your legs. [long pause] Your arms. [long pause] Feel the solidity of what's holding you up.

Notice your hands. [long pause] The warmth or coolness in your palms. [long pause] The slight pulse in your fingertips, if you pay close enough attention. [long pause] You're alive. [long pause] You're here. [long pause] That's where we begin.

Now bring your attention to your chest — the space around your heart. [long pause] Don't analyze what you find there. [long pause] Just notice. [long pause] Is it open? [long pause] Guarded? [long pause] Heavy? [long pause] Calm? [long pause] This is the part of you that will receive the scripture tonight. [long pause] Not your head. [long pause] Your heart.

Tonight we're going to practice something ancient. [long pause] For more than fifteen hundred years — since the early monasteries of the Benedictine tradition — Christians have practiced what they call Lectio Divina. [long pause] Sacred reading. [long pause] It wasn't invented as a technique or a spiritual exercise. [long pause] It was simply the way monks received scripture — slowly, prayerfully, with the expectation that God would speak through it. [long pause] Not information to be mastered, but a living word to be encountered.

Saint Benedict taught his monks to listen "with the ear of the heart." That's what we're doing tonight. [long pause] Not studying. [long pause] Not analyzing. [long pause] Listening — for a word that might be meant specifically for you.

The practice has four movements. [long pause] Lectio — reading. [long pause] Meditatio — meditating. [long pause] Oratio — praying. [long pause] And Contemplatio — resting in God's presence. [long pause] You don't have to remember the names. [long pause] Just follow the movements. [long pause] They'll carry you.

We begin with Lectio. [long pause] Reading. [long pause] Or rather, listening — because in the monasteries, scripture was always read aloud. [long pause] The ear receives differently than the eye. [long pause] Something that passes quickly on a page might stop you in your tracks when you hear it spoken.

I'm going to read a passage from Psalm twenty-seven. [long pause] The first time through, simply receive the words. [long pause] Don't try to catch everything. [long pause] Don't analyze. [long pause] Don't study. [long pause] Just let the words wash over you the way water washes over stone.

"The Lord is my light and my salvation — whom shall I fear? [long pause] The Lord is the stronghold of my life — of whom shall I be afraid? [long pause] One thing I ask from the Lord, this only do I seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to gaze on the beauty of the Lord and to seek him in his temple."
Psalm twenty-seven, verses one and four.

Now I'll read it a second time. [long pause] More slowly. [long pause] And this time, listen with the ear of the heart. [long pause] Pay attention to any word or phrase that draws you — that seems spoken directly to you. [long pause] You might not know why. [long pause] It might surprise you. [long pause] Don't choose the word. [long pause] Let the word choose you. [long pause] The monks trusted that this was the Holy Spirit, gently pointing: here. [long pause] This one. [long pause] This is what you need today.
"The Lord... [long pause] [long pause] is my light... [long pause] [long pause] and my salvation... [long pause] [long pause] "
"The Lord is the stronghold... [long pause] [long pause] of my life... [long pause] [long pause] "
"One thing I ask from the Lord... [long pause] [long pause] this only do I seek... [long pause] [long pause] "
"That I may dwell... [long pause] [long pause] in the house of the Lord... [long pause] [long pause] all the days of my life... [long pause] [long pause] "
"To gaze... [long pause] [long pause] on the beauty of the Lord... [long pause] [long pause] "
"And to seek him... [long pause] [long pause] in his temple."

What word or phrase found you? [long pause] It might be a single word — "light" or "dwell" or "beauty." It might be a phrase — "one thing I ask" or "whom shall I fear." Whatever it is, hold it gently. [long pause] Don't squeeze it. [long pause] Don't immediately start interpreting it. [long pause] Just let it rest in you, the way a seed rests in soil.

Now we move to Meditatio. [long pause] Meditation. [long pause] But not meditation as emptying the mind — this is a filling. [long pause] You're turning the word over in your heart, slowly, the way you'd turn a smooth stone in your hand. [long pause] Feeling its weight. [long pause] Its texture. [long pause] Its warmth.
Stay with the word or phrase that drew you.
Say it to yourself again, silently. [long pause] Let it echo.
What does it stir in you? [long pause] Is there a memory attached to it? [long pause] A face? [long pause] A season of your life?
Does this word touch a wound? [long pause] Something still tender, something that hasn't healed the way you thought it would?
Does it touch a hope? [long pause] Something you barely dare to want, something you've been afraid to ask for?
Does it touch a fear? [long pause] Something you've been carrying quietly, trying not to look at directly?
Does it touch a longing? [long pause] A deep, unnamed desire — for God, for home, for peace, for something you can't quite articulate?

Don't rush past whatever comes. [long pause] If tears come, let them. [long pause] Tears are honest. [long pause] If emotion rises, let it rise. [long pause] If nothing comes — if the word just sits there quietly — that's fine too. [long pause] The word is working in you whether you feel it or not. [long pause] Not everything that matters announces itself.

Where do you feel this word in your body? [long pause] Is there a warmth in your chest? [long pause] A tightness in your throat? [long pause] A settling in your stomach? [long pause] An opening somewhere? [long pause] The word isn't just for your mind — your body is receiving it too. [long pause] Pay attention to what's happening below the neck.
Let me close with a prayer.

Lord, thank you for your word. [long pause] Not as information, but as encounter. [long pause] Not as a lesson to learn, but as a voice to hear. [long pause] Thank you for the word that found me tonight — you know what it means in my life better than I do.

Let it continue its quiet work in me. [long pause] In the busyness of tomorrow, in the noise and the hurry and the hundred things that demand my attention, let it surface at the right moment. [long pause] Let it shape me in ways I can't plan or predict or control.

And when I come back to this practice — tomorrow night, or next week, or months from now — meet me again. [long pause] In the words. [long pause] In the silence between the words. [long pause] In the stillness beyond all words, where you have always been waiting.
I came tonight to listen. [long pause] Thank you for speaking.

When you're ready, gently open your eyes. [long pause] Move slowly. [long pause] There's no rush back into the world. [long pause] The word goes with you. [long pause] The silence goes with you. [long pause] The presence goes with you.
